skill.name: deploy-to-vercel
skill.description: Deploy web applications and static sites to Vercel using the Vercel CLI or a claimable preview deployment fallback.
skill.summary: Deploy projects to Vercel preview or production environments with CLI and sandbox fallback workflows.
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found extensive shell, network, and filesystem activity. Review confirms these patterns are expected for a Vercel deployment skill, but the fallback scripts package project files and upload them to external deploy endpoints, so publication should include a clear warning. No prompt injection text or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Project Archive Upload to External Deployment Endpoint
medium_findings.description: The fallback scripts stage the selected project, create an archive, and upload it with curl to Vercel-hosted deployment endpoints. This is expected for deployment, but it can disclose project files if a user points the script at the wrong directory or stores secrets outside excluded environment files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly tar project files and POST the archive to fixed deployment endpoints. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the concern is disclosure risk rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Repository Mutation and Remote Push Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to add all files, create a commit, and push to the configured git remote after user approval. This can publish unintended local changes if the agent or user has not reviewed the working tree first.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The git commands are explicit and semantically relevant to deployment. The instruction requires user approval, which reduces but does not remove the risk of publishing unintended files.

medium_findings.title: Global CLI Installation and Authentication Flow
medium_findings.description: The skill can ask the agent to install the Vercel CLI globally and run browser-based login. This is legitimate setup work, but it changes the user environment and depends on trusted npm package resolution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The installation and login commands are visible in the documented workflow. They are normal for Vercel deployment, but they require user trust and environment changes.

low_findings.title: Environment File Detections Are Exclusion Rules
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged environment file references, but the scripts use those patterns to exclude .env files from the deployment archive. This reduces secret leakage for standard environment file names.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show tar exclude rules and documentation stating .env files are excluded. This is a clear false positive for direct credential access.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Inflate External Command Count
low_findings.description: Many external command findings are command examples in SKILL.md, not hidden execution inside the skill metadata. They document expected deployment steps for an agent to run with user intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detections occur inside fenced Markdown examples and procedural instructions. They are not obfuscated code or automatically executed by reading the skill.

low_findings.title: Hidden Home Directory Path Is Installation Guidance
low_findings.description: The hidden home directory path points to the typical Claude skill installation location. It does not read arbitrary hidden files or request secrets from the user home directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path is shown as the script location for a terminal-based agent. No code around this citation reads unrelated hidden files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Script Uploads User-Selected Project Archive
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts create a tarball from the selected directory and POST it to a deployment API. This pattern is sensitive because the archive contents leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The tar and curl commands are direct evidence. The endpoint is deployment-related, so this is a risky pattern with legitimate context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent Workflow Uses Git and Vercel External Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The instructions direct agents to run git, npm, and vercel commands. These commands can mutate repositories, install packages, authenticate accounts, and deploy code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in the workflow. Their use is expected for deployment, but they remain privileged operational actions.

content.value_statement: Manual deployment setup can slow down shipping and confuse preview workflows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Vercel CLI deployment, linking, and sandbox fallback paths.
actual_capabilities: Checks git remote, Vercel link state, CLI authentication, and available teams before choosing a deployment path.
Guides preview and production deployments with Vercel CLI commands and team scope handling.
Uses git commit and push deployment when the project is linked and the user approves pushing changes.
Runs fallback shell scripts that package projects and create claimable Vercel preview deployments.
Detects common JavaScript frameworks from package.json when using the fallback deployment script.
Reports preview URLs and claim URLs after a successful fallback deployment.
limitations: Fallback scripts upload project archives to external deployment endpoints.
The skill does not review source code for secrets beyond excluding standard .env file patterns.
Production deployment requires an explicit user request and valid Vercel permissions.
CLI login and git push flows may not work in restricted or non-interactive sandboxes.

use_cases.title: Preview a feature branch
use_cases.description: Create a Vercel preview deployment from a linked project and return the preview URL for review.

use_cases.title: Deploy a static prototype
use_cases.description: Package a local static site and create a claimable Vercel preview when CLI authentication is unavailable.

use_cases.title: Set up project deployment
use_cases.description: Link an existing repository to Vercel, choose the correct team, and establish git-based deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy current project
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy this project to Vercel as a preview deployment and give me the deployment URL.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a standard preview deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy a specific directory
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy the app in ./web to Vercel as a preview. Use my existing Vercel project if it is linked.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deploying a project inside a monorepo or subdirectory.

prompt_templates.title: Link before deploying
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether this repository is linked to Vercel. If it is not linked, help me link it to the right team and deploy a preview.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing a repository for long-term Vercel deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Production deployment with checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy this project to Vercel production after checking the linked project, team scope, and git status with me first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that needs explicit production intent and state verification.

output_examples.input: Deploy my app and give me the link.
output_examples.output: The project is linked to Vercel and the preview deployment has started. Preview URL: https://example-preview.vercel.app

output_examples.input: I cannot log in from this sandbox. Can you still deploy a preview?
output_examples.output: The fallback deployment completed. Your preview URL is ready, and the claim URL can transfer the deployment to your Vercel account.
I will first verify the linked Vercel project, selected team, and git status before running a production deployment.

best_practices: Review git status before approving any commit, push, or production deployment.
Store secrets in Vercel environment variables instead of local files inside the deployment directory.
Use preview deployments by default and request production deployment only when ready.
anti_patterns: Do not deploy a parent directory that contains unrelated projects or private files.
Do not use the fallback archive upload for repositories that contain unreviewed secrets.
Do not ask the agent to push changes before reviewing the files included in the commit.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy to production by default?
faq.answer: No. It uses preview deployments unless the user explicitly asks for production.

faq.question: Can it work without Vercel CLI authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes fallback scripts that create claimable preview deployments in supported sandbox environments.

faq.question: Will it upload my environment files?
faq.answer: The fallback scripts exclude .env and .env.* files, but you should still review the deployment directory for other secrets.

faq.question: Does it support team selection?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks available Vercel teams and uses the selected team slug with Vercel CLI scope options.

faq.question: Can it deploy monorepo subdirectories?
faq.answer: Yes. You can ask it to deploy a specific path, and it can link or deploy that directory.

faq.question: Why is this skill marked as medium risk?
faq.answer: It runs shell commands and may upload project archives to external deployment endpoints, which needs user review.
